Class RangeSegment

java.lang.Object
dev.dejvokep.boostedyaml.dvs.segment.RangeSegment
All Implemented Interfaces:
Segment
public class RangeSegment extends Object implements Segment
Represents an immutable segment constructed from an integer range.

  • Constructor Details

    • RangeSegment

      public RangeSegment(int start, int end, int step, int fill)
      Creates a segment from the given range properties.

      The range is defined by start (inclusive) and end (exclusive) boundaries and difference between each integer called step. To get i-th element of the range, we can use this formula:

      start + step*i

      Filling defines how long (in digits) must each integer in the range be. If an integer in the range has fewer digits than defined by the parameter, additional zeros (0) are prepended to fulfill the requirement (5 -> 005 for fill=3). Set to non-positive (<= 0) value to disable.

      Please mind following:

      • step must not be equal to 0,
      • start cannot be equal to end,
      • step must be specified accordingly to the boundaries, if start < end it must be positive (and vice-versa),
      • all elements in the range must be >= 0, which implies the same for start, but not necessarily for end (which may be negative),
      • if fill > 0, each integer in the range must have at most fill digits (e.g. no 3 digit integer for fill=2); mathematical notation: 0 <= each range integer <= 10fill - 1.
      If any of the conditions above is not met, an IllegalArgumentException is thrown.
      Parameters:
      start - starting boundary (inclusive) of the range, also the first element in the range
      end - ending boundary (exclusive) of the range
      step - difference between each neighboring elements in the range (value needed to add to get from element i to i+1)
      fill - filling parameter (or <= 0 to disable)

    • RangeSegment

  • Method Details

    • parse

      public int parse(String versionId, int index)
      Description copied from interface: Segment
      Parses the given version ID, starting from the provided index and returns the index of element that was matched.

      For LiteralSegment, this is the first element to which String.startsWith(String) returns true. For RangeSegment, this is the shortest sequence of characters at the start which represent a number within this range.

      If no element in the segment is matched, returns -1. This might be the case if the ID is malformed, or if the individual segments overlap each other.

      Specified by:
      parse in interface Segment
      Parameters:
      versionId - the version ID to parse
      index - starting index from which to parse the ID
      Returns:
      index of the first matched element (which appears at the provided index of the version ID)

    • getElement

      public String getElement(int index)
      Description copied from interface: Segment
      Returns i-th element in the segment's definition (the first element having index 0).

      It must apply that 0 <= i < length(), otherwise, an IndexOutOfBoundsException will be thrown.

      Specified by:
      getElement in interface Segment
      Parameters:
      index - the index
      Returns:
      the element at that index

    • getElementLength

      public int getElementLength(int index)
      Description copied from interface: Segment
      Returns length (in characters) of the i-th element in the segment's definition (the first element having index 0).

      It must apply that 0 <= i < length(), otherwise, an IndexOutOfBoundsException will be thrown.

      Specified by:
      getElementLength in interface Segment
      Parameters:
      index - the index
      Returns:
      the length of the element at the given index

    • length

      public int length()
      Description copied from interface: Segment
      Returns the length of the segment - amount of elements in the segment's definition.
      Specified by:
      length in interface Segment
      Returns:
      the length of the segment
